name: Electron
upperStageCapability: Curie kick stage; precise orbital insertion
genericPerformanceNotes: Dedicated small-sat launcher; ~300 kg to 500 km SSO
notes: Electric turbopump-fed Rutherford engines; 1st stage recovery via helicopter attempted

name: Falcon 9 (Block 5)
upperStageCapability: Merlin Vacuum; single restart for GTO/direct-GEO insertion
genericPerformanceNotes: Expendable LEO capacity ~22.8 t; reusable mode ~15.6 t to LEO
notes: Workhorse vehicle; booster reuse up to 20+ flights demonstrated

name: Ariane 6 (A64)
upperStageCapability: Vinci engine; multiple restarts for flexible orbit insertion
genericPerformanceNotes: A64 (4 boosters) ~11.5 t GTO; A62 ~4.5 t GTO

name: Starship / Super Heavy
upperStageCapability: Full second stage is Starship spacecraft; orbital refueling planned
genericPerformanceNotes: Designed for full reuse; expendable figures much higher; refueling enables deep-space payloads
notes: Performance figures are targets; vehicle still in test-flight phase

name: Centaur V (Upper Stage / Space Tug)
upperStageCapability: RL10C-1-1 engine(s); multi-hour coast, multiple restarts, high-energy orbit insertion
genericPerformanceNotes: Upper stage of Vulcan; can deliver ~27 t LEO or ~14.4 t GTO when paired with Vulcan booster
notes: Successor to Centaur III; integral part of Vulcan Centaur stack; tug-like multi-burn capability
